title: morella spathulata
intro: Morella spathulata, also known as the desert paperbark, is a shrub or small tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Morella spathulata include reducing inflammation and fighting infections, as well as potentially treating conditions such as eczema and wounds. Herbal preparations of Morella spathulata include teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a qualified healthcare practitioner.
